front: Film: opens with an extreme close-up of beetles warring in the grass, then the discovery of a severed ear in a field
Film: gas-huffing Frank Booth exclaims 'Heineken? Fuck that shit! Pabst! Blue! Ribbon!' while ritualistically raping nightclub singer Dorothy Vallens as Jeffrey watches from a closet
Film: David Foster Wallace's essay '[Lynch] Keeps His Head' discusses a scene where a woman covers her entire face in red lipstick
Film: theme 'Falling' sung by Julee Cruise (lyrics by Lynch); score by Angelo Badalamenti; co-created with Mark Frost
Film: prequel — Cherubini's C minor Requiem plays as Leland kills Laura Palmer in a train car and wraps her in plastic
back: Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me (David Lynch)

front: Film: revival season features a depiction of the Trinity nuclear test accompanied by Penderecki's Threnody for the Victims of Hiroshima
Film: Dougie Jones is a 'tulpa' of Agent Cooper who becomes trapped in the Black Lodge; played by Kyle MacLachlan; inspired by Peter Sellers in Being There
Film: a mysterious blue box is opened, causing characters (played by Naomi Watts and Laura Harring) to shift identities; a creepy monster emerges from behind a diner
Film: a woman believing herself to be Rita Hayworth discovers the body of Diane Selwyn
Film: John Hurt spent eight hours a day in prosthetics to portray Joseph Merrick; Barber's Adagio for Strings plays as his character dies in his sleep
Film: opens with a giant floating sperm emerging from Henry Spencer's mouth; he and Mary X try to care for and kill a horrifying skinless baby
Director: longtime music collaborator Angelo Badalamenti, first hired to coach Isabella Rossellini's singing for Blue Velvet
Director: wrote the book Catching the Big Fish about creativity and Transcendental Meditation
Director: ordered a chocolate milkshake at Bob's Big Boy diner every day at 2:30 pm for seven years
